clinical_history: 26-year-old man prior to enrolling in anti-convulsive medication trial.  What is your summary interpretation (choose ONE)?
diagnosis: The ECG is within normal limits. Sinus rhythm is present at a rate of about 66/min with normal QRS axis (about +75 degrees) and intervals.  Precordial (chest lead) voltage may appear somewhat prominent but is well within normal range, especially in young adults, without left atrial abnormality or ST-T changes of left ventricular hypertrophy.
